title: Troubleshooting doc, point 10), should create 65536 subuids and subgids, no?
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Pohttps://twitter.com/CityPamS/status/1109514996302528512dman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

/kind bug

<!--
Briefly describe the problem you are having in a few paragraphs.
-->

Setting up podman for first time on Fedora 30 Branched, and discovered I had to manually create subuids and subgids for my account, so I arbitrarily created 10,000 of each, only to have podman tell me I needed 65536 of them. I fixed that, and all worked. So the example in the troubleshooting doc at the very end of point 10), which creates only 1,000 of them, would seem to be doomed to failure, unless I am misreading something.

comments.body: Well we have fixed podman in the upstream to remove the requirement of 65k UIDs.  So when Podman 1.2 ships you should not have this issue.
Ah, gotcha. Should have checked the history, would probably have noticed that.
